title: Barcoding lentiviral Cre vectors for use in experiments involving downstream Tuba-seq analysis.
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"jtcvk","text":"This protocol outlines the procedure to massively barcode Lentiviral Cre vectors. This procedure was developed for use of the Tuba-seq pipeline to calculate tumor sizes in lentivirally inducible mouse models of cancer. The protocol accompanies Rogers et al, Nature Methods, published online May 22, 2017 (nmeth.4297).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: PCR amplify the barcoded insert off of Lenti-PGK/Cre plasmid. Reaction mix: 200 l PrimeStarHS Mix 12 l Forward Primer (10 M) 12 l Reverse Primer (10 M) 8 l Template (500 ng/L) 168 l H20 400 l Total Split into 4, 100 l reactions
Temp. (°C) Time (min) 98 1:00 98 0:15 55 0:05 72 1:00 Go to step 2, 34x 72 5:00 4 forever
Pool the PCR reactions and PCR purify using the Qiagen® PCR Purification Kit. Elute in 35 l H20.
Digest barcoded insert with BspEI and BamHI. Reaction mix: 5 l Buffer 3.1 1.5 l BspEI 1.5 l BamHI 3 g Barcode Insert H20 to 50 l 50 l Total Incubate at 37°C 2 hours
Concurrently digest Lentiviral Plasmid with XmaI and BamHI. Reaction mix: 5 l Cutsmart Buffer 1.5 l XmaI 1.5 l BamHI 5 g Lentiviral Plasmid H20 to 50 l 50 l Total Incubate at 37°C 2 hours
Run out digested products on a 2% agarose gel and gel extract the fragments using the Qiagen® MinElute Gel Extraction Kit (Qiagen, 28604) and elute in 25 l of H20.

Ligate digested barcoded insert into digested Lentiviral Plasmid Reaction mix: 8 l Ligation Buffer 1.6 l T4 DNA Ligase (NEB, M0202) 300 ng Digested Lentiviral Plasmid 180 ng Digested Barcoded Insert H20 to 80 l 80 l Total Incubate at 16°C overnight
PCR purify the ligation product using the Qiagen® PCR Purification Kit (Qiagen, 28106)

Transform 1 l into 20 l ElectroMAX DH10B cells (Thermo Fisher, 18290015). Use 0.1 cm GenePulser/MicroPulser Cuvettes (Bio-Rad, 165-2089) and BD MicroPulser™ Electroporator (Bio-Rad,165-2100)at 1.9 kV.
Ad 500 l of SOC media and shake at 200 rpm for 30 minutes at 37°C.
Plate on LB-Amp plates (1x1 l, 1x 10 l, and 5x 100 l) and incubate at 37°C overnight.

Count colonies on either the 1 l or 10 l plate and use these counts to estimate the colony number on the 100 l plates. The total number of colonies counted will provide an estimate of the number of unique barcodes obtained.
Scrape bacterial colonies off of the LB-Amp plates in 10 mL of LB-Amp per plate.
Pool liquid in a flask and shake at 200 rpm for 30 minutes at 37°C.
Midi-prep the barcoded plasmid pool using the Qiagen® HiSpeed MidiPrep Kit (12643).
Determine plasmid concentration and confirm barcoding by Sanger sequencing.
2-3 full days.

The transformation will be inhibited if there is any salt left in the transformed plasmid solution. You may need to titrate the voltage on the electroporater, depending on the machine they behave differently.
None.
creator: Protocols Importer
